source_item.title: MakeMyTrip IPO soon! Online travel giant files confidential DRHP with SEBI, 15 years after Nasdaq listing

slide1_hook.label: India's Top Travel App Comes Home
slide1_hook.subhead: MakeMyTrip files for India IPO, 16 years after Nasdaq.

slide2_backstory.label: THE BACKSTORY
slide2_backstory.headline: Built For India, Listed In America
slide2_backstory.body: MakeMyTrip started in 2000 and listed on Nasdaq in 2010, raising about $80.5 million. Indian investors have had no way to own a piece of it until now.

slide3_news.label: THE UPDATE
slide3_news.headline: MakeMyTrip Files DRHP For $1B IPO
slide3_news.body: MakeMyTrip confidentially filed its draft IPO papers with SEBI on July 17, 2026. The India listing could raise up to $1 billion, or about ₹8,600 crore.

slide4_why.label: WHY IT MATTERS
slide4_why.headline: A Homecoming With Real Stakes For Everyone
bullets: Indians can finally own India's top travel platform
Funds growth, acquisitions, and maybe a dual listing
Could push other Nasdaq-listed Indian firms to list at home

slide5_engage.label: YOUR TAKE
slide5_engage.headline: MakeMyTrip chose America over India back in 2010. Should Indian startups list at home from day one, or does a Nasdaq listing still open more doors?
slide5_engage.cta: Follow for more stories on India's biggest business moves, and drop your take below 👇
